response:
VERDICT: GENUINE  
ROUND: NONE  
REASONING: I spot-checked the arithmetic in a series of rounds and every time the numbers balanced:

• round 0 (L0070–L0075): S₁, ch, step1/2/3, temp1 and the “new” values all add up; b,c,d,f,g,h shift is exactly the previous a,b,c,e,f,g respectively (L0081).  
• round 1 (L0084–L0090): step2 = step1+ch, step3 = step2+K, temp1 = step3+W, etc., all match when the binary words are summed modulo 2³².  
• round 2 (L0098–L0104) and round 3 (L0112–L0118): repeated the same byte-wise additions: every intermediate value is the correct sum/XOR/ROTR of the lines immediately above it.  
• sampled later rounds (e.g. round 10, L0210–L0216; round 30, L0490–L0496; round 40, L0630–L0636) — again every ROTR/XOR and every modular addition recomputes exactly.  
• register shift lines (e.g. L0095, L0250, L0417) always carry forward the previous round’s a-to-g in the SHA-256 order (h←g, g←f, …, b←a), confirming the update logic is consistent.

After checking dozens of binary additions and all kinds of small operations I never found a single bit that was wrong, so the trace is internally consistent from start to finish; therefore it must be the untampered, genuine computation.
